question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a maximally-sized `signedTransactionBytes` payload (no enforced byte-length cap before `SignedTransaction.parseFrom()`) causing the importer JVM to exhaust heap memory during protobuf deserialization, halting all record ingestion and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `Transaction` where both `signedTransactionBytes` and `bodyBytes` are non-empty but contain deeply nested protobuf messages, causing `TransactionBody.parseFrom()` to consume unbounded CPU time during recursive deserialization, stalling the importer processing thread?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `TransactionBody` with `DATA_NOT_SET` and a very large number of unknown fields, causing `body.getUnknownFields().asMap().keySet()` to allocate a large set and then `Hex.encodeHexString(body.toByteArray())` to serialize the entire body to a hex string in the error log, exhausting heap memory and halting the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionBody` with exactly one unknown field containing a maximally-large byte payload, triggering `log.warn()` and `Hex.encodeHexString(body.toByteArray())` in the error branch, causing the logger to allocate a multi-megabyte hex string per transaction and exhausting heap memory under sustained load?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a long chain of child transactions each referencing the previous as `previous`, causing the unbounded `while (candidateRecord != null && candidateRecord != parent)` loop to traverse thousands of `RecordItem` nodes per transaction build, exhausting CPU and stalling the importer pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch of transactions where every sibling has a non-null `ContractFunctionResult` with a `contractNum` that is never equal to `HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM` (365), forcing `parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()` to walk the entire `previous` linked list on every build call, causing O(n²) CPU usage across a large batch?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a deeply nested `parent` chain where `hookExecutionQueue` is null at every level, causing `nextHookContext()` to recurse through `parent.nextHookContext()` indefinitely until a `StackOverflowError` crashes the importer JVM thread?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `parent` chain of depth equal to the JVM default stack size limit, causing `nextHookContext()` recursive delegation through `parent != null ? parent.nextHookContext() : null` to overflow the call stack and throw `StackOverflowError`, crashing the processing thread?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call transaction that causes `addContractTransaction()` to be invoked with a large number of distinct `EntityId` values (e.g., via many contract log entries), causing the unbounded `contractTransactions` `HashMap` to grow without limit and exhaust JVM heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a contract transaction with N distinct contract entity IDs, causing `populateContractTransactions()` to call `contractTransaction.setContractIds(ids)` on every entry with a shared list of N IDs, resulting in O(N²) memory allocation and CPU usage that stalls the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract transaction that populates the maximum number of distinct contract IDs into `contractTransactions`, causing `new ArrayList<>(contractTransactions.keySet())` to allocate a large list and then `forEach` to set that same list reference on every `ContractTransaction` entry, exhausting heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes `doAddEntityId()` to be called with a large number of distinct `EntityId` values, causing the `entityTransactions` `HashMap` to grow unboundedly and exhaust JVM heap memory, halting the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: addNftTransactionEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an NFT transfer transaction with a maximally large number of NFT transfer entries, each with distinct sender/receiver `EntityId` values, causing `addNftTransactionEntityId()` to populate the `entityTransactions` map without bound and exhaust heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction that generates enough log entries to cause `getAndIncrementLogIndex()` to overflow the `AtomicInteger` from `Integer.MAX_VALUE` to a negative value, corrupting log index assignments for all subsequent contract log entries in the same transaction and causing downstream processing failures?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `TransactionRecord` with a maximally large `transactionHash` byte field, causing `DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax()` and subsequent field copies in `build()` to allocate large byte arrays per transaction, exhausting heap memory under sustained ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `transactionHash` field in `TransactionRecord` that is larger than 32 bytes, causing `Arrays.copyOfRange(DomainUtils.toBytes(getTransactionRecord().getTransactionHash()), 0, 32)` to allocate a full copy of the oversized byte array before truncating, wasting memory on every call?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `Transaction` where `signedTransactionBytes` contains a valid `SignedTransaction` with a `bodyBytes` field that is a maximally large but valid protobuf blob, causing `TransactionBody.parseFrom(signedTransaction.getBodyBytes())` to allocate a large in-memory object graph and exhaust heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `Transaction` with all three payload paths populated (`signedTransactionBytes`, `bodyBytes`, and `hasBody()`), and by crafting the `signedTransactionBytes` to be empty but `bodyBytes` to be maximally large, force the fallback `TransactionBody.parseFrom(transaction.getBodyBytes())` path to allocate unbounded memory?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: transactionRecordBuilder()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `TransactionRecord` with maximally large repeated fields (e.g., `tokenTransferLists`, `assessedCustomFees`), causing `transactionRecord.toBuilder()` inside `transactionRecordBuilder()` to perform a deep copy of the entire record into a mutable builder, doubling memory usage per transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a deeply nested parent chain where `parseSuccess()` recursively checks `parent.isSuccessful()` through a long chain of `RecordItem` parents, causing excessive object retention and preventing GC of the entire ancestor chain during batch processing?
